text: Strength training reduces the risk of acute and overuse sports injuries by approximately 66% (relative risk 0.338).
A 10% increase in strength training volume reduces the risk of sports injury by more than four percentage points, demonstrating a dose-dependent relationship.
specific_value: 10% volume increase → >4 percentage point risk reduction

text: Increasing both strength training volume and intensity are independently associated with sports injury risk reduction.
Strength training for injury prevention is safe, with high compliance rates observed across multiple randomised controlled trials.
Strength training is effective for preventing both acute and overuse injuries across diverse populations and intervention types.
